_comment: Escalation patterns + templates — merged into single file

patterns.description: Use exposed credentials to access protected resources
Escalate to code execution via file write or database injection
Use XSS to steal session tokens and hijack accounts
Extract sensitive data via UNION queries
Execute commands via gadget chain exploitation
Pivot to internal network from compromised host
Escalate cloud access to full account compromise

INJECTION.step: Try escalation via {param} parameter with different encoding
Test for second-order injection in stored data
Check for stack trace disclosure that reveals internals

AUTH.step: Check if session token can be reused across different accounts
Check for OAuth state parameter tampering

ACCESS.step: Check for IDOR in related resources
Test for forced browsing to admin endpoints

DATA.step: Check if sensitive data is cached by CDN/proxy
Test for data exfiltration via response headers
Check for PII in client-side storage

CODE.step: Check for code execution in background jobs
Check for template injection in email

CONFIG.step: Check for debug endpoints in non-standard paths
Test for alternate channel exposure (WebSocket, gRPC)
Check for source code disclosure via backup files

NETWORK.step: Check for internal service fingerprinting via SSRF
Test for DNS zone transfer possibilities
Check for internal port scanning via webhooks

FILE.step: Check for XML external entity in file upload

BUSINESS_LOGIC.step: Test for negative quantity exploitation in checkout flow
Check for race condition in coupon/promo code application
Verify workflow state machine bypass (skip steps)

API_ABUSE.step: Test for rate limit bypass via header manipulation
Check for bulk operation abuse (mass actions)
